document.summary: The dataset provides information on arms imports in the Eastern and Southern Africa region, measured in SIPRI trend indicator values. The data spans from 1994 to 2025, with notable fluctuations in import values over the years. The most recent data point for 2023 indicates arms imports of 166 million USD, a significant decrease from 258 million USD in 2022.
key_takeaways: Arms imports peaked at 1.116 billion USD in 2011.
The value of arms imports has fluctuated significantly over the years.
In 2023, arms imports were recorded at 166 million USD.
The data shows a downward trend in arms imports from 2022 to 2023.
The dataset includes values from 1994 to 2025.
There are several years with missing data.
document.why_it_matters: Understanding arms imports is crucial for assessing military capabilities and security dynamics in the Eastern and Southern Africa region. This data can inform policy decisions and international relations regarding arms trade and regional stability.

claims.claim_text: Arms imports in Eastern and Southern Africa decreased from 258 million USD in 2022 to 166 million USD in 2023.
